meta.title: Frica — African Countries Master Data
meta.description: Master data file for all 54 recognized sovereign African countries. Powers country grids, search, and navigation across the Frica platform.

countries.name: Algeria
countries.short_description: Africa's largest country by area, rich in Saharan landscapes, Berber heritage, and a blend of Arab and Mediterranean culture.

countries.name: Angola
countries.short_description: A nation of stunning landscapes from the Namib Desert to the Congo rainforest, with a vibrant culture shaped by Bantu traditions and Portuguese influence.

countries.name: Benin
countries.short_description: Birthplace of Vodun (Voodoo) religion and once the powerful Kingdom of Dahomey, Benin is a cradle of West African spiritual traditions and art.

countries.name: Botswana
countries.short_description: Home to the Okavango Delta and the Kalahari Desert, Botswana stands as one of Africa's great conservation success stories and stable democracies.

countries.name: Burkina Faso
countries.short_description: Land of the 'Upright People', Burkina Faso is rich in Mossi, Lobi, and Fulani cultures, known for its vibrant festivals and West African film heritage.

countries.name: Burundi
countries.short_description: Nestled in the heart of Africa around Lake Tanganyika, Burundi has a deep tradition of royal drumming recognized by UNESCO as intangible heritage.

countries.name: Cabo Verde
countries.short_description: An island nation off West Africa with a unique Creole culture, famous for Morna music — a soulful genre born from the longing of island life.

countries.name: Cameroon
countries.short_description: Known as 'Africa in miniature' for its incredible diversity — from rainforests to savannas, 250+ ethnic groups, and a legendary football history.

countries.name: Central African Republic
countries.short_description: A land of dense rainforests and the Sangha River basin, home to Aka (Pygmy) people whose polyphonic forest music is a UNESCO treasure.

countries.name: Chad
countries.short_description: Spanning from the Sahara in the north to lush savannas in the south, Chad holds the ancient cultures of the Kanem-Bornu Empire and over 200 ethnic groups.

countries.name: Comoros
countries.short_description: The 'Perfume Islands' of the Indian Ocean, Comoros is a Swahili-Arab-Malagasy blend with an ancient sultanate heritage and unique volcanic beauty.

countries.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
countries.short_description: The heart of Africa — the DRC is home to the world's second-largest rainforest, the Congo River, Soukous music, and unparalleled biodiversity.

countries.name: Republic of the Congo
countries.short_description: Brazzaville faces Kinshasa across the Congo River — one of the world's unique twin-capital spectacles — with a culture deeply rooted in Bantu traditions.

countries.name: Côte d'Ivoire
countries.short_description: The Ivory Coast is West Africa's economic powerhouse — world's top cocoa producer, birthplace of Coupé-Décalé music, and a vibrant cultural hub.
known_for: Basilica of Our Lady of Peace (Yamoussoukro)

countries.name: Djibouti
countries.short_description: A tiny nation at the Horn of Africa guarding the Gulf of Aden, Djibouti is a surreal landscape of salt lakes, volcanic rock, and Afar warrior culture.
known_for: Whale sharks in Gulf of Tadjoura

countries.name: Egypt
countries.short_description: Home to one of humanity's oldest civilizations, Egypt gave the world the Pyramids, hieroglyphics, pharaohs, and a culture that has shaped all of human history.

countries.name: Equatorial Guinea
countries.short_description: Africa's only Spanish-speaking country, Equatorial Guinea spans the mainland and Bioko Island, home to the Fang and Bubi peoples and rich oil wealth.

countries.name: Eritrea
countries.short_description: Asmara, Eritrea's capital, is a showcase of Italian Modernist architecture (UNESCO), while the nation's Red Sea coast holds ancient Aksumite ruins.

countries.name: Eswatini
countries.short_description: Africa's last absolute monarchy, Eswatini is celebrated for the spectacular Incwala and Umhlanga Reed Dance ceremonies that honor Swazi royal tradition.

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.note: Ethiopia was never colonized — one of only two African countries to resist colonization
countries.short_description: One of the world's oldest nations, Ethiopia was never colonized, gave the world coffee, and is home to 9 UNESCO World Heritage Sites including ancient Aksum.

countries.name: Gabon
countries.short_description: One of Africa's greenest nations — 88% covered in rainforest, Gabon is home to forest elephants, mandrills, and unique Bwiti spiritual traditions.

countries.name: Gambia
countries.short_description: Africa's smallest mainland country, Gambia is a sliver of land along the Gambia River, with deep griot storytelling traditions and joyful West African culture.

countries.name: Ghana
countries.short_description: First sub-Saharan African country to gain independence in 1957, Ghana is the home of Kente cloth, Highlife music, the Ashanti Empire, and a proud pan-African legacy.

countries.name: Guinea
countries.short_description: Guinea is the 'water tower of West Africa' fed by the Fouta Djallon highlands, and home to the Mandé people — heart of West African musical heritage.

countries.name: Guinea-Bissau
countries.short_description: A nation of islands, rivers, and mangroves, Guinea-Bissau blends Portuguese-Creole culture with diverse Mandé, Fula, and Balanta traditions.

countries.name: Kenya
countries.short_description: East Africa's economic powerhouse, Kenya blends Swahili coast culture, Maasai warrior traditions, Gikuyu heritage, and world-class wildlife on the Great Rift Valley.

countries.name: Lesotho
countries.short_description: The 'Kingdom in the Sky' — entirely surrounded by South Africa and sitting entirely above 1,000m elevation, Lesotho has a proud Basotho blanket and horse culture.
known_for: Sani Pass (highest paved road in Africa)

countries.name: Liberia
countries.short_description: Africa's oldest republic, founded by freed American slaves, Liberia has a distinct Americo-Liberian history and is home to the Poro and Sande secret societies.
known_for: Palm butter soup & cassava leaves cuisine

countries.name: Libya
countries.short_description: An ancient crossroads of Phoenician, Greek, Roman, Arab, and Berber civilizations, Libya holds the spectacular ruins of Leptis Magna and Cyrene.

countries.name: Madagascar
countries.short_description: The world's fourth-largest island is a biological wonder — 90% of its wildlife exists nowhere else on Earth, alongside Malagasy-Austronesian-African culture.

countries.name: Malawi
countries.short_description: The 'Warm Heart of Africa' — Malawi's Lake Malawi holds more fish species than any other lake, surrounded by Chewa, Yao, and Tumbuka cultural richness.

countries.name: Mali
countries.short_description: Heir to three great West African empires — Ghana, Mali, and Songhai — Mali gave the world Timbuktu's ancient manuscripts, the Griot tradition, and Blues music roots.

countries.name: Mauritania
countries.short_description: A vast desert nation where the Sahara meets the Atlantic, Mauritania is home to ancient Berber-Arab Moorish culture and the UNESCO caravan city of Chinguetti.

countries.name: Mauritius
countries.short_description: A rainbow-nation island of Indian, African, Chinese, and European heritage, Mauritius is famous for its Sega music, Creole cuisine, and the extinct Dodo bird.
known_for: Crystal Mountain & Chamarel Seven Colored Earths

countries.name: Morocco
countries.short_description: The gateway between Africa and Europe, Morocco enchants with its imperial cities, Sahara dunes, Berber mountain villages, Gnawa music, and ancient medinas.
known_for: Fes el-Bali (world's largest car-free urban area)

countries.name: Mozambique
countries.short_description: With 2,500km of Indian Ocean coastline, Mozambique blends Portuguese-Bantu-Arab Swahili heritage with the vibrant Marrabenta music and Makonde wood sculpture.

countries.name: Namibia
countries.short_description: Home to the world's oldest desert, Namibia is a stark and breathtaking land of the Himba people, the Skeleton Coast, and the towering red dunes of Sossusvlei.

countries.name: Niger
countries.short_description: A vast Saharan and Sahelian nation, Niger is home to the ancient Air Mountains, Tuareg silver craftsmanship, and the W National Park biosphere.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.short_description: Africa's most populous nation and largest economy, Nigeria is the beating heart of Afrobeats music, Nollywood film, Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa-Fulani civilizations.

countries.name: Rwanda
countries.short_description: The 'Land of a Thousand Hills' has become Africa's model of governance and conservation, home to mountain gorillas, Intore dancers, and the Imigongo art tradition.

countries.name: São Tomé and Príncipe
countries.short_description: A tiny island nation in the Gulf of Guinea, São Tomé blends Portuguese-Creole culture with Ussua and Socopé dances in a lush equatorial paradise.

countries.name: Senegal
countries.short_description: West Africa's cultural crown — Senegal gave the world Mbalax music, the philosophy of Teranga (hospitality), Gorée Island's slave history, and Sufi brotherhood culture.

countries.name: Seychelles
countries.short_description: 115 islands of granite and coral in the Indian Ocean, Seychelles is the home of the Coco de Mer — the world's largest seed — and a rich Creole island culture.

countries.name: Sierra Leone
countries.short_description: Home of the Temne and Mende peoples, Sierra Leone's Freetown was founded for freed slaves and is known for its Bubu music, diamond history, and white-sand beaches.

countries.name: Somalia
countries.short_description: With the longest coastline in mainland Africa, Somalia has a proud ancient history as the 'Land of Punt', and a rich oral poetry and Quranic scholarship culture.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.short_description: The 'Rainbow Nation' — 11 official languages, the Cape Winelands, Zulu warrior history, Nelson Mandela's legacy, Amapiano music, and the Cape of Good Hope.

countries.name: South Sudan
countries.short_description: The world's youngest country (2011), South Sudan is home to the Nilotic Dinka and Nuer peoples, vast Sudd wetlands, and one of Africa's great wildlife migrations.

countries.name: Sudan
countries.short_description: Sudan has more ancient pyramids than Egypt — heir to the Nubian Kushite kingdoms, Meroe, and a culture where Arab, Nubian, and African traditions interweave.

countries.name: Tanzania
countries.short_description: Home to Mount Kilimanjaro (Africa's highest peak), Zanzibar's Swahili spice island culture, the Serengeti, and the oldest known human footprints at Laetoli.

countries.name: Togo
countries.short_description: A thin strip of West Africa with a stunning diversity of landscapes from coast to mountain, Togo is home to the Ewe, Kabyè, and Mina peoples with deep Vodun roots.
known_for: Marché des Féticheurs (voodoo fetish market, Lomé)

countries.name: Tunisia
countries.short_description: The northernmost African country sits where the Roman Empire, Carthage, and Arab-Berber civilizations left their mark — from the Sahara to the Mediterranean.

countries.name: Uganda
countries.short_description: The 'Pearl of Africa' (Churchill's words), Uganda is home to mountain gorillas, Lake Victoria, the Baganda Kingdom, Buganda culture, and the source of the Nile.

countries.name: Zambia
countries.short_description: Home to one of the Seven Natural Wonders — Victoria Falls (Mosi-oa-Tunya), Zambia is a land of 73 Bantu ethnic groups, Bemba royalty, and the Kuomboka ceremony.

countries.name: Zimbabwe
countries.short_description: Great Zimbabwe's ancient stone city gave this nation its name — a proud Shona and Ndebele civilization with Mbira music, Victoria Falls, and Hwange wildlife.
